title: example-data

options.label: P1
options.author: llama3:latest
options.text:
Here's an example of defining a small data structure and operations on it:

```scheme
(define-struct Point (x y))

(define-struct Vector ((start Point) (end Point)))

(define (vec-length v)
  (+ (abs (- (vector-end-x v) (vector-start-x v)))
     (abs (- (vector-end-y v) (vector-start-y v)))))

(define (vec-mag v)
  (sqrt (+ (sqr (abs (- (vector-end-x v) (vector-start-x v))))
           (sqr (abs (- (vector-end-y v) (vector-start-y v)))))))

(define-struct Circle ((center Point) (radius Number)))

(define (circle-perimeter c)
  (* 2 pi (circle-radius c)))

(define (circle-area c)
  (* pi (expt (circle-radius c) 2)))
```

This example defines a simple data structure for points, vectors, and circles. It also includes some basic operations on these data structures: calculating the length and magnitude of vectors, as well as the perimeter and area of circles.

options.label: P2
options.author: deepseek-r1:8b
options.text:
(define (make-point x y)
  (cons x y))

(define (x point) (car point))
(define (y point) (cdr point))

(define (distance-from-origin point)
  (sqrt (+ (square (x point)) (square (y point)))))

(define (distance-between point1 point2)
  (sqrt (+ (square (- (x point1) (x point2))) (square (- (y point1) (y point2))))))

(define p1 (make-point 3 4))
(define p2 (make-point 0 0))

(distance-from-origin p1)  ; 5
(distance-between p1 p2)   ; 5

options.label: P3
options.author: mistral:7b
options.text:
```scheme
; Define a simple list data structure with append (cons) and length operations
(define empty-list '())

(define (make-list values)
  (if (null? values)
      empty-list
      (cons (car values) (make-list (cdr values)))))

(define (append-list l1 l2)
  (if (empty-list? l1)
      l2
      (cons (car l1) (append-list (cdr l1) l2))))

(define (length-list list)
  (if (empty-list? list)
      0
      (+ 1 (length-list (cdr list)))))

; Example usage: create and manipulate a list of numbers
(display (make-list '(1 2 3 4))) ; Output: (1 2 3 4)
(display (append-list '(5 6) '(7 8))) ; Output: (5 6 7 8)
(display (length-list '(a b c d e f g h))) ; Output: 8
```

options.label: P4
options.author: qwen3:8b
options.text:
```
(define (make-tree val left right)
  (list val left right))

(define (tree-value tree)
  (car tree))

(define (tree-left tree)
  (cadr tree))

(define (tree-right tree)
  (caddr tree))

(define (count-nodes tree)
  (if (eq? tree '())
      0
      (+ 1 (count-nodes (tree-left tree)) (count-nodes (tree-right tree)))))

(define my-tree (make-tree 1 (make-tree 2 '() '()) (make-tree 3 '() '())))
(count-nodes my-tree)  ; evaluates to 3
```

options.label: P5
options.author: gemma4:latest
options.text:
```scheme
;; Define a simple Pair data structure (Key-Value) using the list primitives.
;; A pair is represented as the list (Key Value).

;; 1. Constructor: Creates a Pair (Key -> Value)
(define (make-pair key value)
  (cons key value))

;; 2. Accessor: Retrieves the Key from a pair
(define (get-key pair)
  (car pair))

;; 3. Accessor: Retrieves the Value from a pair
(define (get-value pair)
  (cdr pair))

;; 4. Update Operation: Returns a *new* Pair with the updated value.
;; This respects immutability by constructing a new list.
(define (set-value pair new-value)
  (cons (get-key pair) new-value))

;; Example Usage:
(define user-record (make-pair "user_id" 101))
(display "Original Record: ")
(display user-record) ; Expected output: (user_id 101)
(newline)

(display "Retrieved Key: ")
(display (get-key user-record)) ; Expected output: user_id
(newline)

(display "Original Value: ")
(display (get-value user-record)) ; Expected output: 101
(newline)

;; Update the record value
(define updated-record (set-value user-record 202))
(display "Updated Record: ")
(display updated-record) ; Expected output: (user_id 202)

;; Demonstrate immutability: original record remains unchanged
(display "Original Record (Check): ")
(display user-record) ; Expected output: (user_id 101)
(newline)
```

winner_text:
```scheme
;; Define a simple Pair data structure (Key-Value) using the list primitives.
;; A pair is represented as the list (Key Value).

;; 1. Constructor: Creates a Pair (Key -> Value)
(define (make-pair key value)
  (cons key value))

;; 2. Accessor: Retrieves the Key from a pair
(define (get-key pair)
  (car pair))

;; 3. Accessor: Retrieves the Value from a pair
(define (get-value pair)
  (cdr pair))

;; 4. Update Operation: Returns a *new* Pair with the updated value.
;; This respects immutability by constructing a new list.
(define (set-value pair new-value)
  (cons (get-key pair) new-value))

;; Example Usage:
(define user-record (make-pair "user_id" 101))
(display "Original Record: ")
(display user-record) ; Expected output: (user_id 101)
(newline)

(display "Retrieved Key: ")
(display (get-key user-record)) ; Expected output: user_id
(newline)

(display "Original Value: ")
(display (get-value user-record)) ; Expected output: 101
(newline)

;; Update the record value
(define updated-record (set-value user-record 202))
(display "Updated Record: ")
(display updated-record) ; Expected output: (user_id 202)

;; Demonstrate immutability: original record remains unchanged
(display "Original Record (Check): ")
(display user-record) ; Expected output: (user_id 101)
(newline)
```
